description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by several dark metal hoops or bands that encircle the barrel at regular intervals. The top surface is flat and appears to be a fitted wooden lid, secured by the same metal banding, with faint engraved or stamped text visible. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it an aged and rustic appearance. On the side, there are stenciled markings reading 'RED HEAD WHISKEY,' indicating its likely use for storing or aging whiskey. The overall build suggests it is robust and suitable for storage or transport of liquids in urban or industrial settings.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and stenciled 'RED HEAD WHISKEY' markings, featuring a flat wooden lid and a rustic, aged appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stenciled 'WHISKEY' marking and a small circular bunghole on the side.
description_view_1: Side view with no visible markings, highlighting the wood grain and metal hoops.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with plain wood and metal bands visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the full 'RED HEAD WHISKEY' stenciled marking across the barrel's side.
